nativeRange.habitat: Aquarium-trade hybrid cultivar, not found in the wild. Parent Echinodorus species originate from South American marshes, river margins, and seasonally flooded wetlands.

decorPreferences: root tab supplementation near (not under) the crown
open background space for fan-shaped growth
moderate to bright lighting to emphasize sparkle pattern

imageCandidates.notes: Retailer/care-guide page with photographs of Sparkle Sword showing the characteristic lighter blotches and sparkly leaf appearance; useful as a visual reference for reviewers to source a freely-licensed equivalent.

summary: The Sparkle Sword is an aquarium-trade Echinodorus cultivar prized for broad, oval leaves marked with lighter blotches that catch the light and give the plant its characteristic shimmering appearance. A beginner-friendly background plant for medium and larger freshwater tanks.
careNotes: Sparkle Sword is a HYBRID CULTIVAR of Echinodorus that does not occur in the wild; like other named Echinodorus hybrids such as 'Ozelot' and 'Rose', it is propagated through the aquarium trade. The defining feature is broad, oval leaves marked with lighter blotches that catch the light, producing the iridescent sparkle effect for which it is named. Adult height is typically around 30 cm (approximately 12 inches) under good conditions, and occasionally larger. CO2 is not required but accelerates growth and increases leaf size. This is a HEAVY ROOT FEEDER: a nutrient-rich substrate is strongly recommended, and root tabs should be placed near (not directly under) the crown when using inert gravel or sand. Keep the crown just above the substrate line to prevent rot. Best used as a background plant — in tanks shorter than 60 cm it will tend to dominate the layout. The broad leaves provide useful natural cover and spawning surfaces for community fish. Newly planted specimens may shed their emersed-form leaves as they convert to submerged growth; this is normal, and new submerged leaves will follow.
breedingNotes: Propagates like other Echinodorus cultivars by adventitious plantlets ('daughter plants') that develop on long flower stalks emerging from the crown, plus occasional side runners. Once each plantlet has a small rosette and visible roots it can be cut from the stalk and planted in the substrate. Mature crowns can also be carefully divided. True seed production is uncommon in submerged aquarium setups.

primary.name: Modern Aquarium — Sparkle Sword
primary.notes: Retailer/care-guide page for Echinodorus sp. 'Sparkle'. Provided South American parentage, ~12 in (~30 cm) height, moderate growth, moderate light (stronger light boosts growth and fullness), CO2 not required but beneficial, temperature 68–82 °F (20–28 °C), nutrient-rich substrate with root tabs, midground-to-background placement (background in smaller tanks), runner/plantlet propagation, easy/beginner-friendly difficulty, and the distinctive lighter-blotched 'sparkle' leaf appearance. pH and hardness were not specified.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Echinodorus
additional.notes: Genus reference used to confirm taxonomy (family Alismataceae, order Alismatales), American native range of parent species, marsh/bog habitat that grows submersed, and the broader context that many named Echinodorus aquarium-trade forms are cultivars or hybrids — supporting the 'Sparkle' cultivar designation.
